publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because Formal conformance requires a pinned standards validator and interpretation of its findings; that service is not installed in the offline verifier.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Markup conformance: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents misconception? If a page looks correct in one browser, HTML conformance errors cannot matter.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Validate authored and rendered HTML, fix structural errors, and still perform accessibility and content review afterward.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Validate authored and rendered HTML, fix structural errors, and still perform accessibility and content review afterward.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Validate authored and rendered HTML, fix structural errors, and still perform accessibility and content review afterward.
